data.content:
The build confirms the issue. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. Glob-based implicit includes expand at **evaluation time** (before targets run), so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and track for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file during the build (before `CoreCompile`), but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit globbing happens at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, after it's been created. I should also add it to `<FileWrites>` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me fix the .csproj file.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and track for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** MSBuild's implicit `<Compile>` glob expands at *evaluation time* — before any targets execute. The `GenerateSampleCode` target creates the `.cs` file at *execution time*, so it's never seen by the compiler.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="..."/>` and `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` inside the target's `<ItemGroup>` so the generated file is explicitly included in compilation and properly cleaned on `dotnet clean`.
